title: Plain-language family renditions of published cards (education-only, not medical advice)
context: The patient/family plain-language layer is HIGH risk-tier and dual-gated: each rendition is education only, never medical advice, sourced to its parent card's citations, and ships ONLY with both an oncologist and a patient-advocate sign-off. It carries the persistent 'this is research information, not medical advice - talk to your (or your child's) oncology team' frame, gives no individualized risk/prognosis/treatment guidance, and is withheld if either sign-off is missing or stale. Reviewers (oncologist + advocate) are TO BE SECURED; if not secured this layer does not ship and the research-only library stands as the deed. Binding cancer guardrails apply: only open-access / aggregate / de-identified sources (controlled-access - dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks - and any identifiable patient data are out of scope); per-source license verified before reuse (COSMIC/OncoKB cite-only, CIViC CC0, DepMap CC-BY, ClinVar public domain, PMC-OA per-article); no-source-no-claim provenance on every assertion. No partner/reviewer/steward is yet secured, so this task carries requestor 'TO BE SECURED' and verifiedNeed false.
objective: Draft one plain-language family rendition per published, domain-reviewed research card - education only, sourced to its parent card's citations, carrying the not-medical-advice frame, meeting the readability/tone and WCAG 2.2 AA standards, with no individualized guidance, and published only with dual oncologist + patient-advocate sign-off (withheld otherwise).
acceptanceCriteria: One plain-language rendition per published, domain-reviewed research card; each is sourced to its parent card's citations and carries the persistent 'this is research information, not medical advice - talk to your (or your child's) oncology team' frame
Meets a readability + tone standard: no false hope (no cure/efficacy promises), no false despair, uncertainty shown plainly, no individualized risk/prognosis/treatment guidance
Both an oncologist and a patient advocate sign off every card (dual, version-scoped, recorded); COI disclosed/recused as needed (reviewers TO BE SECURED)
Accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA) verified; cards withheld (not auto-published) if either sign-off is missing or stale
